sect1.title: Introduction
sect1.para: The Grift class, implemented in Cython, represents a specific implementation of a Graph Neural Network (GNN) model within the AnalysisG framework. It inherits from the generic ModelTemplate class, extending its functionalities to incorporate features and structures relevant to the Grift architecture. This class provides a high-performance interface to an underlying C++ GNN model, enabling efficient training and inference for graph-based data.

sect1.title: The Grift Class
sect1.para: Grift is a cdef class, indicating its implementation as a Cython extension type. It inherits from ModelTemplate, thereby gaining all the base functionalities for managing input/output graph features, device settings, and checkpoint paths. Its primary role is to encapsulate a C++ grift object, which likely contains the core GNN model logic and parameters.

listitem.para: __cinit__(self): The Cython "constructor" equivalent, called during object allocation. It initializes a new C++ grift object and assigns its pointer to self.rnn. It then assigns self.rnn to self.nn_ptr, allowing Grift to be treated as a generic ModelTemplate while still managing its specialized C++ object.
__init__(self): The standard Python initializer. This method is intentionally left empty, as initialization logic is handled by __cinit__ and the underlying C++ object.

__dealloc__(self): The Cython "destructor" equivalent, called upon object deallocation. It deallocates the C++ grift object pointed to by self.rnn, ensuring proper memory management for the specialized model.

sect2.title: Properties
sect2.para: These properties provide controlled access to specific parameters and configurations of the Grift GNN model.

listitem.para: @property xrec -> int: Gets and sets the _xrec parameter of the underlying C++ grift object. This likely relates to the number of recurrent steps or input features.
@property x -> int: Gets and sets the _xin parameter of the underlying C++ grift object. This likely relates to the input feature dimension.
@property is_mc -> bool: Gets and sets the is_mc flag of the underlying C++ grift object. This likely indicates whether the model is processing Monte Carlo data.
@property drop_out -> double: Gets and sets the drop_out rate of the underlying C++ grift object. This is a regularization parameter.
@property PageRank -> bool: Gets and sets the pagerank flag of the underlying C++ grift object. This likely enables or disables PageRank-related features within the model.

sect1.title: C++ Interaction
sect1.para:
The Grift class leverages Cython's capabilities for seamless C++ integration:
cdef class Grift: Declares Grift as a Cython extension type.
cdef grift *rnn: A C++ pointer to a grift object, which is the specialized C++ implementation of the GNN model.
self.nn_ptr = self.rnn: This line demonstrates how the base ModelTemplate's C++ pointer (nn_ptr) is made to point to the specialized grift object, allowing Grift to fulfill the ModelTemplate interface.
from AnalysisG.core.model_template cimport ModelTemplate: Imports the base ModelTemplate class, from which Grift inherits.
from AnalysisG.models.Grift.grift cimport *: Imports the C++ grift class declaration from its .pxd file.
